design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels using bladed boomerangs and unlockable spell attacks, culminating in boss encounters at level ends
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Speed-focused action platforming with brutal difficulty—player must master weapon timing and spell usage while maintaining momentum
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying Anett Myer, a young Peruvian sorceress wielding elemental magic to prevent an ancient cult from awakening the god Hastur
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty design paired with fast-paced gameplay; refusal to soften challenge for accessibility; emphasis on player skill execution over narrative hand-holding

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Spell attacks provide tactical options but core challenge remains execution-based; no documented difficulty settings

progression.upgrade_system: Progressive spell unlocks (up to five spell attacks available across the campaign)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Spells unlocked through level progression; bladed boomerangs available from start as infinite resource
replayability_hooks: Challenging difficulty encourages mastery and speedrun potential
Spell variety invites experimentation with different attack combinations

world_building_techniques: H.P. Lovecraft Cthulhu Mythos references embedded in plot
Historical setting (1920s New York, Al Capone era) provides atmospheric grounding
Shared fictional universe with Earnest Evans trilogy creates continuity
Character motivation (preventing Hastur's awakening) frames all action

boss_design.philosophy: Each level culminates in a boss fight; bosses serve as skill gates testing player mastery of acquired spell attacks and boomerang timing

faction_indicators: Cult leader Henry and sorceress Restiana represent antagonistic supernatural faction
Al Capone (Vincente DeMarcoto in localization) represents organized crime element
Anett Myer and Earnest Evans form protagonist alliance
danger_signals: Hastur awakening plot signals apocalyptic threat
Cult activities indicate organized supernatural danger
Ancient bloodline descendants (Anett) marked as targets
environmental_storytelling: 1920s New York setting with anachronistic elements (denim-clad bikers with scimitars) creates surreal, tone-mixing atmosphere
Level design implicitly tied to preventing cult ritual sites
